title: Percentage to Double Conversion
prompt_md:
## Goal

Give a `Percentage` class (which stores an integer percent) an **explicit** conversion operator to `double` that yields the decimal form -- e.g. a `Percentage` of 75 converts to `0.75`.

## Input

- One integer `P` (0 <= P <= 100).

## Output

- The decimal equivalent printed with exactly 2 decimal places, followed by a newline.

The conversion must be written as `explicit operator double() const` (a member function, no parameters, no separate return type). The provided `main` reads `P`, builds a `Percentage`, and prints `static_cast<double>(...)` with `std::fixed` / `std::setprecision(2)`.

title: Boolean Validity Check
prompt_md:
## Goal

Give an `ID` class (which stores an integer identifier) an **explicit** conversion to `bool`: an `ID` is valid (true) when its value is positive, invalid (false) when zero or negative. Thanks to the special rule for `operator bool`, an explicit `operator bool()` may still be used directly in conditionals.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `N` (1 <= N <= 50).
- Line 2: `N` space-separated integers (-1000 <= each <= 1000).

## Output

- For each integer, print `valid` if the corresponding `ID` is valid, else `invalid`, one per line.

Write `explicit operator bool() const`. The provided `main` constructs an `ID` from each value and uses it directly in an `if` statement (no cast, no getter).
